chunks.text: Ph.D. Teaching Requirement - EECS at Berkeley Ph.D. Teaching Requirement For a comprehensive explanation of policy please refer to The Graduate Handbook . The EECS department requires all Ph.D. candidates to serve as a Graduate Student Instructor (GS

ates to serve as a Graduate Student Instructor (GSI) within EECS. The GSI teaching requirement not only enhances and helps to develop a student’s communication skills, but it also makes a great contribution to the department’s academic community. If

bution to the department’s academic community. If you are planning a career in academia, your GSI experience will prove a strong addition to your CV. The requirement is defined as 30 hours*. Practically speaking this will mean one 50% appointment (a

lly speaking this will mean one 50% appointment (averaging 20hrs/wk) and one 25% appointment (averaging 10hrs/wk). 20 of these hours need to be in EE or CS undergraduate courses Before your first GSI assignment you must attend the Teaching Conference

assignment you must attend the Teaching Conference for First-TIme GSI’s through the Teaching and GSI Resource Center . You must complete a 375 pedagogy course either before or concurrent to your GSI assignment It is possible to petition to transfer s

ssignment It is possible to petition to transfer some previous TA work you’ve done. We allow a maximum of 20 hours to be transferred. Guide for Prospective ASEs (Academic Student Employees) Guide for Current ASEs < Ph.D. Qualifying Examination Ph.D.
